name: Upper Back (Trapezius & Rhomboids)

landmarks.notes: Tolerates high volume and frequency; receives substantial indirect work from rows, deadlifts, and carries. The 24-26 MRV is a model-based upper-bound estimate, not a directly measured ceiling — high-volume dose-response data are sparse. The cited Enes 2024 set-progression trial is on the lower limb (quadriceps), not this muscle, and even there found no significant hypertrophy difference between constant and progressively higher-volume training (only a possible small benefit at higher volumes, held with low certainty). Treat this ceiling as uncertain and individual.

frequency_notes: ~2-3x/week; overlaps heavily with lat and rear-delt training.
training_notes: Rows train the mid-traps/rhomboids; add shrugs or upright variations for upper traps if prioritized.
